priorities: Zero‑tolerance for reputational risk from labor or governance scandals
Strict supply‑chain and labor‑rights compliance in all holdings
Data‑driven cost‑of‑capital and asymmetric tail‑risk analysis
Alignment of portfolio risk/return with his own brand’s integrity
Liquidity matching for tax liabilities and opportunistic cash deployment
redLines: Any holding linked to sweatshops, illegal exploitation or wage theft
Companies under live media or judicial scrutiny for labor violations
Positions that could trigger a public boycott or negative Swiss press coverage
Failure to dump a contaminated position before it becomes public
preferredSectors: Blue‑chip consumer discretionary & apparel with stable compliance ratings
Healthcare, technology and energy exposures that meet governance standards
Swiss cantonal short‑term fixed income for liquidity
avoidedSectors: Consumer‑discretionary firms with poor supply‑chain governance
Logistics or manufacturing companies relying on unmonitored cheap foreign labor
esgFocus: Real‑time labor‑rights and supply‑chain governance monitoring
Live media/judicial risk flags rather than retrospective ESG scores
Corporate governance transparency and anti‑exploitation policies

investmentBehavior.timeHorizon: medium‑to‑long term (5‑10 years) with focus on preserving brand reputation over pure return
investmentBehavior.liquidity: Requires moderate liquidity for tax‑liability matching, art purchases and rapid cash redeployment

lifeEvents.description: Added a non‑negotiable mandate to screen all consumer‑discretionary holdings for sweatshop and labor‑exploitation risks.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Introduced strict ESG screening and real‑time monitoring of supply‑chain governance.

lifeEvents.description: Launched a sustainability marketing campaign for his retail brand and accepted lower yields for a clean portfolio footprint.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Willingness to sacrifice performance for reputational cleanliness.

lifeEvents.description: Requested detailed fixed‑income duration to align bond ladder with corporate tax liabilities due late 2025.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Liquidity and duration matching became a priority.

lifeEvents.description: Stated that any international consumer/retail brand hit with severe labor‑exploitation allegations must be dumped immediately.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Reinforced zero‑tolerance red line and need for rapid divestment mechanisms.

lifeEvents.description: Switched idle cash into higher‑yielding Swiss cantonal short‑term instruments.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Optimized cash yield while maintaining safety and liquidity.

lifeEvents.description: Rejected standard ESG scores, demanding live media/judicial alerts for any labor‑practice concerns.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Shifted screening approach to real‑time risk alerts rather than static ESG ratings.

personalProfile.interests: sustainable retail, corporate governance, art collecting
personalProfile.quirks: always cross‑references portfolio data with his own retail metrics; demands granular, table‑based analysis
personalProfile.rapportTriggers: discussion of supply‑chain integrity, sustainability campaigns, corporate‑governance forums
personalProfile.avoidTopics: competitor banks, negative press about his own brand

keyQuotes: My national brand equity is built on clean labor and local integrity. If my name is linked via my portfolio to an international corporate parent caught using sweatshops, illegal exploitation, or wage theft, it creates immediate hypocrisy risk for my own stores.
I will gladly accept a lower Sharpe ratio or lower yields in exchange for a clean portfolio footprint.
If any international consumer or retail brand we hold gets hit with severe labor exploitation allegations, dump the entire position before the local Swiss press can link my name to it.

Standard glossy ESG reporting and backward‑looking sustainability scores are useless to me. I don't care if a company has an 'AA' ESG rating on paper—if there is live media or judicial smoke surrounding their labor practices right now, the position must be flagged immediately before it hits mainstream national press.
My investment portfolio must be a fortress of clean corporate governance. No reputational liabilities, period.
